q:
Number of compounds from the following which will not produce orange red precipitate with Benedict solution is........
Glucose, maltose, sucrose, ribose,2-deoxyribose, amylose, lactose
question:
Number of compounds from the following which will not produce orange red precipitate with Benedict solution is........
Glucose, maltose, sucrose, ribose,2-deoxyribose, amylose, lactose
solution:
Except sucrose and amylose all others, give orange red precipitate with Benedict solution.
Sucrose is a non-reducing disaccharide composed of glucose and fructose. It does not have a free aldehyde or ketone group and, therefore, cannot undergo oxidation and reduce the Benedict's reagent. As a result, sucrose will not produce an orange-red precipitate with the Benedict's solution.

Amylose is a polysaccharide composed of multiple glucose units linked together. While glucose, a monosaccharide, is a reducing sugar, the glycosidic bonds in amylose prevent the free aldehyde or ketone group from being available for oxidation. As a result, amylose, as a part of starch, does not give a positive Benedict's test and will not produce an orange-red precipitate.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which one of the following pairs is an example of polar molecular solids?
question: Which one of the following pairs is an example of polar molecular solids?
solution:
SO2 is a bent molecule with a central sulfur atom bonded to two oxygen atoms. The oxygen atoms are more electronegative than sulfur, causing a separation of charge. This results in a bent molecular geometry and a net dipole moment, making SO2 a polar molecule.

NH3 is a trigonal pyramidal molecule with a central nitrogen atom bonded to three hydrogen atoms. Again, the electronegativity difference between nitrogen and hydrogen leads to a separation of charge. The arrangement of the atoms in NH3 gives it a net dipole moment, making it a polar molecule.

If Ni2+ is replaced by Pt2+ in the complex NiCl2Br22-, which of the following properties are expected to get changed?
A. Geometry
B. Geometrical isomerism
C. Optical isomerism
D. Magnetic properties
question:
If Ni2+ is replaced by Pt2+ in the complex NiCl2Br22-, which of the following properties are expected to get changed?
A. Geometry
B. Geometrical isomerism
C. Optical isomerism
D. Magnetic properties
solution:
When Ni2+ is replaced by Pt2+ in the complex NiCl2Br22-, the following properties are expected to be changed:

A. Geometry: Since Pt2+ and Ni2+ have different coordination preferences and atomic sizes, the replacement is likely to result in a change in the geometry of the complex. That is it changes to square planar.

B. Geometrical isomerism: If the replacement of Ni2+ with Pt2+ leads to a change in the ligand arrangement, it can result in the generation of cis and trans isomers.

C. Magnetic properties: PtCl2Br22- is diamagnetic.

Both the complex species are optically inactive.

An intensive property is a bulk property, meaning that it is a physical property of a system that does not depend on the system size or the amount of material in the system.

ΔG=−nFEcell ​

Statement II: Oxide ores in general are easier to reduce.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: In the metallurgy process,sulphide ore is converted to oxide before reduction.
Statement II: Oxide ores in general are easier to reduce.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
solution:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.

In many metallurgical processes, sulphide ores are first converted to oxide ores through a process known as roasting or calcination. The conversion of sulphide ore to oxide ore is done to remove the sulphur and simplify the subsequent reduction process.

Oxide ores are generally easier to reduce compared to sulphide ores. This is because oxygen is already bonded to the metal in oxide ores, making it easier to remove the oxygen and extract the metal through reduction reactions.

Statement I: Ethane at 333 to 343 K and 6-7 atm pressure in the presence of AlEt3 and TiCl4 undergoes addition polymerization to give LDP.
Statement II: Caprolactam at 533-543 K in H2O through step growth polymerizes to give Nylon 6.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution: Low Density Polyethylene is obtained by the polymerisation of ethene under high pressure of 1000-2000 atm at 350-570 K in the presence of an initiator. High Density Polyethylene is obtained when polymerisation is done in the presence of Ziegler-Natta catalyst at 333-343K under 6-7 atm pressure. Nylon-6 is manufactured from the monomer called caprolactam. The monomer caprolactam is heated at 533-543Kin an inert atmosphere, it polymerises to give nylon-6.

q: Alkali metal from the following with least melting point is
question: Alkali metal from the following with least melting point is
solution:
On moving down the group in alkali metals, the melting point generally decreases. 

As you move down the alkali metal group (Group 1 of the periodic table), the atomic size or atomic radius increases. This increase in atomic size leads to a decrease in the strength of metallic bonds between the alkali metal atoms. Weaker metallic bonding results in lower melting points because less energy is required to overcome these weaker bonds and convert the solid metal into a liquid state.

B. For 2s orbital, the probability density first increases to maximum and then decreases sharply to zero.
C. Boundary surface diagrams of the orbitals encloses a region of 100% probability of finding the electron.
D. p and d-orbitals have 1 and 2 angular nodes respectively
E. probability density of p-orbital is zero at the nucleus
solution:
Probability density curves for 1s, 2s and 2p orbitals are given below.

For 1s orbital, the probability density is maximum at the nucleus. For 2s orbital, the probability density first decreases and then increases. At any distance from nucleus the probability density of finding electron is never zero, and it always have some finite value. The number of angular nodes are equal to azimuthal quantum number. For p-orbital it is equal to 1 and for d-orbital it is equal to 2. Probability density of p-orbital is zero at the nucleus

A. The catalyst is diffused over the surface of reactants.
B. Reactants are adsorbed on the surface of the catalyst.

question:
The number of correct statements about modern adsorption theory of heterogeneous catalysis from the following is ......
A. The catalyst is diffused over the surface of reactants.
B. Reactants are adsorbed on the surface of the catalyst.
C. Occurrence of chemical reaction on the catalyst's surface through formation of an intermediate.
D. It is a combination of intermediate compound formation theory and the old adsorption theory.
E. It explains the action of the catalyst as well as those of catalytic promoters and poisons.
solution:
Adsorption theory of Heterogeneous catalysis involves five steps-
  1. Diffusion of reactants to the surface of the catalyst.
  2. Adsorption of reactant molecules on the surface of the catalyst. (Occurrence of chemical reaction on the catalyst’s surface through formation of an intermediate.
  3.  Desorption of reaction products from the catalyst surface 
  4. Making the surface available again for more reaction to occur.
  5.  Diffusion of reaction products away from the catalyst’s surface.
So, we can deduce from Adsorption theory of catalysis that as the concentration of reactants at active centres increases due to adsorption and thus the rate of the reaction is also increased.

Volume, Molar heat capacity, molarity, Ecello,Gibbs free energy change, Molar mass, Mole
question:
The total number of intensive properties from the following is...........
Volume, Molar heat capacity, molarity, Ecello,Gibbs free energy change, Molar mass, Mole
solution:
An intensive property is a physical quantity whose value does not depend on the amount of substance which was measured.

Molar heat capacity, molarity, Ecello and molar mass are intensive properties.

An extensive property of a system depends on the system size or the amount of matter in the system. 

Volume, Gibbs free energy change, Mole are extensive properties.

For a∈ℂ, let A={z∈ℂ:Re(a+z¯)>Im(a¯+z)} and B={z∈ℂ:Re(a+z¯)<Im(a¯+z)}. Then among the two statements:
(S1) : If Re(a),Im(a)>0, then the set A contains all the real numbers
(S2) : If Re(a),Im(a)<0, then the set B contains all the real numbers,
question:
For a∈ℂ, let A={z∈ℂ:Re(a+z¯)>Im(a¯+z)} and B={z∈ℂ:Re(a+z¯)<Im(a¯+z)}. Then among the two statements:
(S1) : If Re(a),Im(a)>0, then the set A contains all the real numbers
(S2) : If Re(a),Im(a)<0, then the set B contains all the real numbers,
solution:
Let a=x1+iy1, a¯=x1-iy1

And z=x2+iy2, z¯=x2-iy2      

Now,

a+z¯=x1+x2+iy1-y2

and a¯+z=x1+x2+iy2-y1

Now according to the question,

Re(a+z¯)=x1+x2 and

Im(a¯+z)=y2-y1

A=z:x1+x2>y2-y1=z:x1+y1+x2>y2
  B=z:x1+x2<y2-y1=z:x1+y1+x2<y2

If  y2=0 and x1,y1>0 then

A={z:x2>-x1+y1}

A covers a part of negative real axis and therefore, does not contain whole real axis

Similarly if y2=0, and x1,y1<0, then 
B={z:x2<-x1+y1}
∴ B covers part of positive real axis and therefore does not cover whole real axis.

Hence, both are false.

Put z=x+iy in the given expression and equate the imaginary part to zero.

⇒z2+8iz-15z2-3iz-2=x+iy2+8ix+iy-15x+iy2-3ix+iy-2

=x2-y2-8y-15+i2xy+8xx2-y2+3y-2+i2xy-3x

=x2-y2-8y-15x2-y2+3y-2+2xy+8x2xy-3x+i2xy+8xx2-y2+3y-2-i2xy-3xx2-y2-8y-15x2-y2+3y-22-2xy-3x2

But Imz2+8iz-15z2-3iz-2=0,

⇒-x2-y2-8y-152xy-3x+2xy+8xx2-y2+3y-2=0

⇒x2-y22xy+8x-2xy+3x+8y+152xy-3x+2xy+8x3y-2=0

⇒11x3-11xy2+16xy2-24xy+30xy-45x+6xy2-4xy+24xy-16x=0

⇒11x3+11xy2+26xy-61x=0

⇒11x2+11y2+26y-61=0

∵ α≠0

So, put y=-1311, x=α
11α2+11·132112-26·1311-61=0
⇒121α2=840

⇒242α2=1680

Hence this is the required answer.

Now we know that, for infinite solution D=D1=D2=D3=0

 711α54717519457=0

If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given:

dydx+5x1+x5y=1+x52x7

This is linear differential equation.

I.F.=e∫5x1+x5dx

⇒I.F.=e∫5x5x61+x5dx

⇒I.F.=e∫5x6x-5+1dx

⇒I.F.=e-∫-5x6x-5+1dx

⇒I.F.=e-∫dx-5+1x-5+1

⇒I.F.=e-logex-5+1

⇒I.F.=x-5+1-1=x5x5+1

So, solution is given by

yx5x5+1=∫x5x5+1×x5+12x7dx

⇒yx5x5+1=∫x5+1x2dx

⇒yx5x5+1=∫x3+1x2dx

⇒yx5x5+1=x44-1x+C

Since, y1=2, so

22=14-1+C⇒C=74

So,

yx5x5+1=x44-1x+74

Put x=2, then we get

y2×3233=164-12+74

⇒y2=21×33128=693128

solution:
Given equation of ellipse is x236+y29=1

It is of the form x2a2+y2b2=1

With a=6, b=3.

The centre of the circle is C2,0.

Let Pacosθ,bsinθ be the common point for ellipse and circle.

P≡6cosθ,3sinθ

Equation of normal to the ellipse will be axcosθ-bysinθ=a2-b2.

⇒6xsecθ-3ycosecθ=36-9

But the normal through P passes through the centre of the circle C2,0.

⇒62secθ-30cosecθ=27

⇒secθ=2712

⇒cosθ=49 and sinθ=659

P≡6×49,3×659

P≡83,653

Now CP=r=2-832+0-6532

⇒r2=699

⇒12r2=12×699=92

Therefore, the required answer is 92

If the height of the tower is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> meters, then the distance (in meters) between the two persons is equal to
question: The angle of elevation of the top <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> of a tower from the feet of one person standing due south of the tower is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></msup></math> and from the feet of another person standing due west of the tower is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>30</mn><mo>°</mo></msup></math>. If the height of the tower is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> meters, then the distance (in meters) between the two persons is equal to
solution:
Given that the elevation to the top of the tower from the feet of one person due south is =45°

Similarly the angle of elevation to the top of tower from the feet of another person to the west of tower is =30°

The given information can be represented in the form of a diagram as below:

We need to find the distance AB.

Let the distance of A,B from the tower be x1,x2.
⇒tan 45°=5x1, tan 30°=5x2
⇒x1=5, x2=53.

⇒AB=x12+x22=52+532=10 m

Hence this is the correct option.

Energy of Hydrogen like atoms is, E=-13.6Z2n2eV.

Since Helium is in the first state, Z=2, n=2.

The energy of He+ ion is,

E=-13.6×2222=-13.6 eV

solution:
The charge enclosed is given by qinc=CV

⇒20=10+2 r
⇒2r=10
⇒r=5 Ω

question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math> is the gravitational potential due to sphere of uniform density on its surface, then its value at the centre of sphere will be:
solution:
The magnitude of gravitational potential of a solid sphere at a distance r from the centre is 

V=-GM2R3(3R2-r2)

At r=R, V=-GMR.

At r=0, V'=-GM2R3(3R2)=-3GM2R

Clearly, V'=3V2.

q: A space ship of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>4</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is launched into a circular orbit close to the earth surface. The additional velocity to be imparted to the space ship in the orbit to overcome the gravitational pull will be (if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> and radius of earth <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>6400</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi></math> ):
question: A space ship of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>4</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is launched into a circular orbit close to the earth surface. The additional velocity to be imparted to the space ship in the orbit to overcome the gravitational pull will be (if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> and radius of earth <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>6400</mn><mo> </mo><mi>km</mi></math> ):
solution:
The orbital velocity of satellite is 

vorb=GMR

The escape velocity is 

vesc=2GMR

Hence, the additional velocity required is 

Δv=vesc-vorb=2GMR-GMR=(2-1)GMR
=(2-1)gR
=(2-1)10×6400×103
=8(2-1) km s-1

solution:
The RMS value of a gas is given by 

vrms=3RTM=3kBTm

Substituting the given values,

vrms=3×1.4×10-23×3004.6×10-26
≈523 m s-1

q: A body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> moves along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis such that it's velocity varies with displacement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> according to the relation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> the force acting on the body is:
question: A body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>500</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> moves along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis such that it's velocity varies with displacement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> according to the relation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>v</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> the force acting on the body is:
solution:
It is given that 

 v=10x m s-1

Force is defined by F=mdvdt   ...(i)

Multiplying and dividing equation (i) by dx

F=mdvdtdxdx⇒F=mdxdtdvdx=mvdvdx   ...(ii)

Finding the values of the terms in equation (ii)
dvdx=102x=5x
vdvdx=a=10x×5x=50 m s-2

Hence, the force is 
F=12×50=25 N.
